figures.caption: Figure 3: Estimated power of various methods to detect correlated pleiotropy (r = 0) based on 10, 000 replicates. There are no balanced pleiotropic IVs (q = 0).

Figure 4: Estimated power of various methods in the presence of directional and correlated pleiotropy based on 10, 000 replicates. The total proportion of directional and correlated pleiotropic IVs is set at π3 = 1.5%, and there are no balanced pleiotropic IVs (q = 0).
Table 1: Estimated type one error rates (%) of various methods at nominal significance level 0.05 based on 10, 000 replicates.
Figure 1: Causal model with SNP Gj , exposure X, outcome Y , and unmeasured confounder U .

Table 2: Application of various methods to detect directional and/or correlated pleiotropy in MR analysis. # IVs: Counts of selected IVs (average across all pairs). µ̂γ : Mean of selected γ̂js (average across all pairs).
Figure 2: Estimated power of various methods to detect directional pleiotropy (r = 1) based on 10, 000 replicates. There are no balanced pleiotropic IVs (q = 0).
